key_insight: An unsupervised domain adaptation model with gradual source domain expansion can transfer weld penetration state prediction across very different welding processes, including TIG and laser welding.
engineering_relevance: This reduces the need for costly relabeling when deploying intelligent weld monitoring to new processes and improves robustness under process-to-process domain shift.
method: Train a UDA classifier for penetration-state recognition across source and target welding domains.
Use gradual source domain expansion to widen source coverage during adaptation.
Evaluate same-process and cross-process transfer between TIG and laser datasets.
evidence_signal: The method achieved 90.65% on TIGFH and 90.72% on LSPS in same-process tests, and 80.48% TIG→Laser plus 81.13% Laser→TIG in cross-process transfer, outperforming the supervised baseline by about 35.8% to 43.4%.
watch_item: Performance depends on the similarity and quality of the available unlabeled target data, so further validation is needed before deployment to other welding setups or sensing conditions.
paper_title: A cross-process welding penetration status prediction algorithm based on unsupervised domain adaptation in laser and TIG welding
